relative_clauses_complex_relative_clause.name: relative clauses complex relative clauses all levels

questions.question: Choose the relative pronoun: The analyst {a1} prepared the figures is absent today.
Choose the non-defining relative pronoun: My brother, {a1} lives nearby, often helps us.
Choose the relative word for possession: This is the writer {a1} books changed the course.
Choose the pronoun that comments on the whole previous fact: She missed the deadline, {a1} surprised everyone.
Choose the formal relative phrase: This is the issue {a1} we disagreed.
Choose the informal pattern: That is the person I spoke {a1} yesterday.
Choose the relative pronoun: The report, {a1} several experts had reviewed, was published today.
Choose the phrase for people: Ten students joined the project, {a1} had worked abroad.
Choose the reduced active clause: People {a1} nearby often walk to the station.
Choose the reduced passive clause: The documents {a1} yesterday are on your desk.
Choose the correct pronoun after a comma: The plan was approved, {a1} helped the whole team.
Choose the natural short pattern: The topic we talked {a1} yesterday is still important.
